signal.reasoning: high-credibility source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.signal_briefing: Similar to Oct 2023 when Iran attacked Israel — oil spiked 5% intraday before settling. Defense stocks rallied on escalation risk while airlines dropped on fuel cost concerns. Watch for further retaliation.

notes: Validated: OPEC+ April hike headline confirmed and market response matched predicted volatility spike + risk repricing. Attempted calming signal was interpreted as insufficient versus conflict-related supply/transit risks.

evidence_pack.summary: OPEC+ supply increase headline failed to suppress panic premium; tape repriced on transit/security risk.

Hormuz blockade risk dominated marginal supply adjustment
Market skepticism around deliverability/spare capacity in conflict context

signal.title: Oil prices forecast to jump despite Opec+ pledge to raise output
signal.reasoning: high-credibility source, strong historical pattern match.
signal.signal_briefing: March 2020 oil price war crashed crude 65% in days. More recently, OPEC+ increases have pushed oil lower. Energy stocks typically underperform 1-3% on supply additions. Airlines get relief.

notes: Strongly validated: market ignored near-term OPEC+ calming narrative and repriced immediately on physical supply/transit disruption risk. Forecasted jump despite output pledge played out as predicted.

evidence_pack.summary: Supply pledge was treated as delayed/insufficient; real-time disruption risk dominated price discovery and cross-asset repricing.
drivers: OPEC+ +206k bpd seen as lagged and too small versus immediate risk
metrics: OVX and energy options tail-risk pricing elevated
sector_impact: Energy complex strong; inflation-sensitive sectors repriced
Electricity/compute-cost sensitivity concern for render-heavy businesses

signal.title: Retail investors shun private credit funds after Blue Owl gating
signal.reasoning: high-credibility source, strong historical pattern match.

notes: Validated (crisis-event variant): retail-flow/liquidity squeeze dynamics played out in private credit after Blue Owl gating + loan-sale actions. Signal worked as a sentiment-contagion analog to squeeze mechanics (in reverse direction).

evidence_pack.summary: Retail withdrawal pressure and gating shock triggered liquidity-premium repricing and volatility clustering across private-credit-linked names.
drivers: Blue Owl ~1.4-1.5bn loan sale to institutions
OBDC II redemption gating/permanent restriction signal
Retail confidence shock across semi-liquid private credit products
metrics: Sector fundraising down ~40% over late-Feb window
OWL volume spike (~226% vs avg) with sharp swings
Borrow costs rose for related public vehicles/managers
sector_impact: Alt-asset managers and listed private-credit proxies volatile
Liquidity-premium repricing across retail private-credit complex
